data.title: BNB Surpasses 620 USDT with a 1.82% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 13, 2026, 19:22 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 620 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 620.159973 USDT, with a narrowed 1.82% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Hedge Fund Citadel Maintains Employee Pay Despite Lower Returns
data.description: Employee compensation at Ken Griffin's Citadel remained steady last year, even as returns decreased. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant expenses associated with managing large multistrategy hedge funds, especially in a competitive environment for talent acquisition. Despite the challenges, Citadel's decision to uphold pay levels reflects the firm's commitment to retaining skilled professionals in a demanding market.

data.title: Peru's Economy Poised to Benefit from Commodities Boom in 2026
data.description: The chief executive of a major Peruvian bank expressed confidence in the country's economic prospects, anticipating that the mining-driven economy will continue to thrive amid a commodities boom in 2026. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this optimism persists despite the potential disruptions from upcoming general elections and ongoing political instability. The executive's outlook suggests that the economic benefits from the commodities sector may outweigh the challenges posed by the nation's political landscape.

data.title: Former Palantir Engineer Resigns Over Ethical Concerns
data.description: Alex Bores, a former engineer at Palantir Technologies, resigned from his position in 2019 due to ethical concerns regarding the company's collaboration with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Bores' moral objections to Palantir's involvement with ICE, which ultimately led to his decision to leave the company. Bores' departure underscores the ongoing debate over corporate responsibility and ethical considerations in technology partnerships with government agencies.

data.title: If the CFTC “only does Bitcoin,” why did it just invite crypto’s biggest CEOs into the room?
data.description:
CFTC Chair forms a new Innovation Advisory Committee packed with crypto, exchange, and prediction-market CEOs Most crypto traders barely think about the Commodity Futures Trading Commission until something breaks, a lawsuit hits, or a Bitcoin futures headline crosses their feed.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/cftc-chair-forms-a-new-innovation-advisory-committee-packed-with-crypto-exchange-and-prediction-market-ceos/

data.title: Bitcoin faces oversight shift as SEC details Project Crypto
data.description:
SEC testimony outlines temporary guidance under SEC Project Crypto as Congress drafts a federal framework, clarifying token taxonomy and licensing impacts.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-faces-oversight-shift-as-sec-details-project-crypto/

data.title: Brazil Advances Bill to Build 1-Million Bitcoin Sovereign Reserve
data.description:
A proposal to create a Sovereign Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (RESBit) is moving through Brazil’s Congress, and the latest draft significantly expands the original scope. Instead of allocating up to 5% of international reserves, the revised version calls for the gradual acquisition of at least 1,000,000 Bitcoin over five years.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/brazil-advances-bill-to-build-1-million-bitcoin-sovereign-reserve/

data.title: Grant Hill Discusses Transition from NBA Player to Franchise Owner
data.description: Grant Hill, a former NBA Hall of Famer, has transitioned from playing on the court to owning a franchise. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Hill's insights into how his experience as a player influences his approach to building a team. Hill emphasizes the importance of culture, leadership, and legacy in shaping a successful franchise. His unique perspective as a former player provides valuable insights into the dynamics of team ownership and management.

data.title: BlackRock Enters DeFi with $2.2 Billion Tokenized Treasury Deployment
data.description:
BlackRock has made a significant move into decentralized finance (DeFi) by deploying approximately $2.2 billion through tokenized treasury funds on UniswapX. According to NS3.AI, this marks BlackRock's first direct exposure to DeFi governance tokens, highlighting a growing interest in the sector.

Market expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts are increasing, with a 30% probability of cuts before April and over 80% before June. This anticipation is influencing market dynamics, as investors adjust their strategies in response to potential monetary policy changes.

In parallel, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have experienced $410 million in outflows, coinciding with Bitcoin's decline below $66,000. This has raised concerns about institutional sell-offs and heightened market volatility, as investors remain cautious amid these developments.

data.title: S&P 500 Companies' Q4 EPS Performance Falls Below Historical Averages
data.description: In the fourth quarter, 74% of S&P 500 companies have surpassed earnings per share (EPS) estimates, FactSet posted on X. This performance is lower than the five-year average of 78% and the ten-year average of 76%. The data highlights a trend where fewer companies are exceeding expectations compared to previous years, indicating potential challenges in the current economic environment.

data.title: First Brands Considers Chapter 7 Bankruptcy for Some Units
data.description: First Brands and certain creditors are contemplating moving some of its divisions into Chapter 7 bankruptcy liquidation due to dwindling cash reserves, according to individuals familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial challenges faced by the company as it explores potential restructuring options. The decision comes amid ongoing financial difficulties, prompting discussions on the best course of action to address the company's liquidity issues.

data.title: Bitcoin Rally Faces Potential Halt with $150 Million Sell Orders
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that sell orders totaling $150 million in Bitcoin have been placed within the price range of $70,000 to $75,000. This significant volume of sell orders suggests that sellers are prepared to halt the ongoing Bitcoin rally. The placement of these orders indicates a potential resistance level that could impact Bitcoin's upward momentum. Market participants are closely monitoring these developments to assess the future trajectory of Bitcoin prices.

data.title: Trump Media’s ETF Push Isn’t About Crypto Returns — It’s About Political Distribution Power
data.description:
The recent initiative by Trump Media to launch an exchange-traded fund (ETF) is not primarily focused on generating returns from cryptocurrency investments. Instead, its main objective appears to be to establish and enhance political influence and distribution power. By utilizing the ETF as a financial instrument, Trump Media aims to mobilize resources, attract supporters, and leverage the growing interest in digital currencies as a means to amplify its political agenda and strengthen its standing within the political landscape. Ultimately, this strategy reflects a broader effort to intertwine financial mechanisms with political aspirations, prioritizing influence over mere financial gain.
source: https://blocknews.com/trump-medias-etf-push-isnt-about-crypto-returns-its-about-political-distribution-power/

data.title: US federal judge sentences Praetorian’s CEO to 20 years over massive $200 million Bitcoin fraud
data.description:
Ramil Ventura Palafox just got 20 years in federal prison. He ran a fake crypto company called Praetorian and scammed over 90,000 people across the world. A judge in Alexandria, Virginia, sentenced him after he got hit with wire fraud and money laundering charges. Ramil is 61 and holds passports from both the United States […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-federal-judge-sentences-praetorians-ceo/

data.title: Former CEO of The Dolphin Company Arrested in Mexico
data.description: The former CEO of The Dolphin Company, an aquatic-parks operator that has declared bankruptcy, has been arrested in Mexico, according to law enforcement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the legal developments surrounding the company's leadership. The arrest marks a significant turn in the ongoing financial and legal challenges faced by the company.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Diverging Climate Strategies of Global Superpowers
data.description:
The world's leading superpowers are adopting distinct approaches to climate change. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the contrasting strategies of the United States and China in addressing environmental challenges. The U.S., under the leadership of President Donald Trump, has focused on deregulation and boosting fossil fuel production, while China is investing heavily in renewable energy and aiming to become a global leader in green technology.

These divergent paths reflect broader geopolitical dynamics, with each nation prioritizing its economic interests and strategic goals. The U.S. administration's policies have sparked debate domestically and internationally, as critics argue that they undermine global efforts to combat climate change. Meanwhile, China's commitment to renewable energy is seen as a strategic move to enhance its influence in the global market.

The implications of these differing strategies are significant, affecting international relations and the global economy. As both countries continue to pursue their respective paths, the impact on climate change initiatives and global cooperation remains uncertain. Observers are closely monitoring how these policies will shape the future of environmental efforts worldwide.
